address: Trai Mat St, Da Lat 670000 Vietnam
description: The temple grounds have a dragon of length 49 m, the dragon enclosure is made of 12,000 beer bottles, and the dragon mouth covers the Buddha. In front of the temple grounds is a 37 m high seven-storeyed tower, which is considered the highest temple bell tower in Vietnam. In the heart of the tower is a 4.3 m high bell that is considered the heaviest bell in Vietnam; it is 2.33 m wide, weighs 8,500 kg, and was cast in 1999.

reviews.comment: A very unusual temple that will definitely be remembered
I’m a tour guide in South Vietnam (Mui Ne, Mekong, Saigon, Da Lat); Linh Phuoc Pagoda is visitors’ favorite.
Beautiful location, charming and unique for visiting Vietnam.
The Glass Magic of Dalat: Fortunes, the Bell of Desire and 18 Circles of Hell 🐲🔔 If you are in Dalat, Linh Phuoc Pagoda.

address: 3 Huynh Thuc Khang Street, Ward 4, Da Lat, Lam Dong, Vietnam., Da Lat 670000 Vietnam
description: Crazy House (Hang Nga Villa) is a one-of-a-kind architectural wonder nestled in the heart of Da Lat, Vietnam. Designed by architect Dang Viet Nga, Crazy House offers visitors an extraordinary experience through a labyrinth of surreal, organic structures inspired by nature and dreams. Recognized as one of the Top 10 most bizarre buildings in the world, Crazy House is a must-visit attraction for travelers seeking something truly unique. Visitors are invited to explore the twisting stairways, themed rooms, sky bridges, and whimsical gardens that make this place a living piece of art. Ticket Prices: Children under 1.2 meters: Free From 1.2 meters to under 1.4 meters: 30,000 VND From 1.4 meters and above: 80,000 VND Crazy House is open daily from 08:30 AM to 06:30 PM. Come and experience a dreamlike world that you won't find anywhere else!

reviews.comment: A must see when in Da Lat
Crazy House or Madame Natasha's Crazy House

address: 3-5-7 Mai Anh Dao St, Da Lat 670000 Vietnam
description: Just as true love has to surpass several obstacles, the Valley of Love in Dalat had to surpass many challenges in the past in order to gain its rightful name today. In the beginning, the valley was called Vallée D’amour (Valley of Love) by the French before being renamed The Valley of Piece. In 1935, its beauty and captivation persuaded people to once again name it after the most wonderful thing in the world: Love. Thanks to being the honeymoon destination of newly-married couples, this valley is even regarded as Niagara Fall of Vietnam. The Valley of Love today is located 4 miles away from the center of Dalat, the little Paris, on Phu Dong Thien Vuong St.

location_name: St. Nicholas Cathedral of Da Lat
address: 15 D. Tran Phu, Dalat 67000 Vietnam
description:
The St. Nicholas Cathedral (Vietnamese: Nhà thờ Chính tòa Đà Lạt, French: Cathédrale Saint-Nicolas) also called St. Nicholas of Bari Cathedral (Nhà thờ Chính tòa Thánh Nicola Bari) is the cathedral of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Đà Lạt, suffragan of the Archdiocese of Ho Chi Minh City (alternatively still called Saigon), located in Da Lat, the capital of Lam Dong province in the Central Highlands of Vietnam. 

Originally built as a parish church in 1920 — to replace an even older church that had been built in 1917 — under Fr. Frédéric Sidot and subsequently rebuilt in 1922, the Cathedral was constructed by the French in 1931–1932 in an eclectic style Romanesque. Archbishop Colomban Dreyer – the Apostolic Delegate to Indochina – laid the cornerstone on 19 July 1931. On 14 November 1934, the cross that carries the brass weathercock 
was installed at the top of the spire of the central bell tower. The interior was not completed until 1942. The Cathedral was blessed in February of 1942.  
There was a European cemetery around the church, which is no longer in use.
There are five masses every Sunday.
